resource_tradeoff: Shared-memory stage fusion improves load efficiency but introduces bank conflicts and higher register pressure.
lesson: A strong first edit can plateau when it consumes resources or only fixes the easiest local symptom.

resource_tradeoff: Initial host-side cleanup does little; later shared-memory staging and divergence-free thread mapping unlock the real bottleneck.
lesson: A weak first step can still lead to the best trajectory if the branch discovers structural headroom.

resource_tradeoff: Loop reorder targeted 57% global load efficiency, but the metric did not move and speedup stayed below baseline.
lesson: A prominent profiler metric is not necessarily causal.

resource_tradeoff: Single-pass histogram removes second pass and allocation traffic, but first grid has too few blocks; grid tuning balances parallelism and atomic contention.
lesson: Do not treat a bad first implementation as proof that the direction is bad.

resource_tradeoff: Shared memory reduces global reads and expf calls, but occupancy collapses and bank conflicts appear.
lesson: Reducing traffic can lose when the real lever is latency hiding and occupancy.

resource_tradeoff: Block-per-slice increases per-slice parallelism, improving occupancy and reducing memory stalls without reducing read passes.
lesson: For memory-latency-bound kernels, increasing outstanding work can beat reducing bytes.

resource_tradeoff: Online softmax reduces a global read pass, but increases register pressure and never beats simple launch tuning.
lesson: A clean algorithmic story can have less causal leverage than a simple hardware knob.

resource_tradeoff: Changing BLOCK_SIZE to 512 improves scheduler utilization; later tweaks are mostly noise.
lesson: Simple launch configuration can be the highest-leverage optimization.

resource_tradeoff: Double-to-float reduces traffic and occupancy pressure; warp-aligned blocks improve coalescing; XTILE tuning improves bandwidth and scheduling.
lesson: Long-horizon prompting works when the first move enables later moves.

resource_tradeoff: Padding reduces shared-memory bank conflicts but increases shared-memory footprint, lowering occupancy and exposing memory stalls.
lesson: Fixing one metric can be neutralized by another resource cost.
